_comment: Dependency traps — packages that look like bloat but break the device when removed. 'exceptions' are packages that appear in debloat-list.json yet must stay installed: 01-debloat.sh SKIPs them, 02-regression-fixes.sh restores them, 99-verify-all.sh asserts them. 'mustStayEnabled' are packages NOT in the debloat list that must never be disabled/uninstalled either way. Each entry records the symptom you get when you violate it, because every one of these was learned the hard way.

exceptions.reason: The Camera app's last-shot preview thumbnail fires an intent with BOTH pkg and cmp pinned to com.coloros.gallery3d/com.oppo.gallery3d.app.ViewGallery. With the package uninstalled-for-user the tap is a silent no-op (no chooser, no toast) and no default-gallery setting can fix it. Restore: adb shell cmd package install-existing com.coloros.gallery3d. It is only invoked from the camera preview and will not take over as default gallery elsewhere.

mustStayEnabled.reason: Owns the widget-catalog ContentProvider (com.oplus.pantanal.ums.card.configuration.provider). Disabled, the lockscreen 'Add widget' picker spins forever — PantanalClient inits with loadTimeout=-1.
Owns the Epona IPC bus used by Pantanal; same lockscreen widget-picker hang as pantanal.ums.

Lockscreen widget plugin ('InspirationWidget' in logs) that talks to Pantanal. Needed alongside pantanal.ums + uiengine — enabling it alone is not sufficient, but disabling it breaks lockscreen widgets too.

OPPO App Market — the delivery channel for download-on-demand first-party apps (e.g. IR Remote, com.oplus.consumerIRApp, which is NOT preinstalled and cannot be fetched any other way without sideloading).
